cold_open.hook: Warren Buffett's absence is so loud that Berkshire Hathaway's team is basically playing Weekend at Bernie's with a $773 billion portfolio.

markets.macro_note: Chip stocks cratering, small caps getting hammered, VIX spiking—this is what happens when the AI hype machine needs a service.

big_story.story_title: Powell's legacy as Fed chair is fighting inflation and Trump. He may lose the battle against both.
big_story.angle: Powell built his credibility on inflation-fighting, but Trump's tariff threats and fiscal chaos are about to make the Fed look like it's trying to bail out a sinking ship with a teaspoon—and the market knows it.

quick_hits.angle: 30-year Treasury yield hits 5.1%—highest in a year—because apparently the bond market has decided the US debt load is a feature, not a bug.
Starbucks laying off 300 US employees and hinting at more; this is what happens when your stock has commitment issues and your CEO needs to prove he's serious.
British Gas pays £20m for force-fitting prepayment meters on vulnerable customers—regulatory theater that costs just enough to feel like punishment but not enough to change behavior.
Cerebras stock pulls back after blowout IPO opening—the AI chip darling just learned that 'best opening day' and 'sustainable valuation' are not the same sentence.

odd_thing.joke_angle: A Trump arcade game that's actually good is the most unhinged timeline flex—it's like finding out a NFT project has real utility.

yesterday_callback.topic: Cisco just posted record quarterly revenue while firing 3,900 people—the most profitable layoff in corporate theater
yesterday_callback.fresh_take: Starbucks is now running the same playbook: layoffs as a stock-price reset button. Cisco proved it works; Starbucks is betting the market will applaud the encore.

sign_off.callback_target: Buffett's absence at Berkshire Hathaway—the cold open's Weekend at Bernie's bit
